question:
Will Athletic Club vs. Qarabağ Ağdam FK end in a draw?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for October 22, 2025
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".

Resolution source: https://www.uefa.com/

They'll be eager to bounce back, and their home ground could be the perfect place to do it. Expect them to come out firing, looking to control the game with their midfield and exploit any gaps in Qarabağ's defence. evidences.content: ‌ Manchester Evening News: Number one for news, opinion, sport & celebrity news Latest from the World Cup 2026 here Sport Football Manchester United FC # Athletic Bilbao latest injury and suspension news before Man United second leg - 'We'll see' ## Latest Manchester United news as the Europa League semi-final second leg against Athletic Club de Bilbao moves ever closer Comments Sport Kieran King Football Writer 08:35, 05 May 2025 Athletic Club de Bilbao head coach Ernesto Valverde has hinted that Oihan Sancet and Nico Williams could be involved against Manchester United on Thursday. The La Liga side, who are 3-0 down from the first leg, travel to Old Trafford in the second leg of their Europa League semi-final. ‌ [...] In terms of injuries, Sancet, who missed the first leg with a muscle issue, is fighting to get back fit and could return to face United in the second leg. Meanwhile, Spain star Williams played 79 minutes in Bilbao before being substituted, though there were concerns over his fitness beforehand. Now, Valverde has offered a fresh update on Sancet and Williams going into this Thursday's second leg. Speaking after Athletic Club were held to a goalless draw against Re...

evidences.title: Athletic head coach: We treat Qarabagh with respect | Baku.ws News Site - Latest News and Events
evidences.content: Baku.ws Baku.ws Baku.ws Select language az ru en instagram Baku.ws Baku.ws About us # Athletic head coach: We treat Qarabagh with respect Athletic head coach: We treat Qarabagh with respect We have a chance to earn points in the match with "Qarabag". As reported by BAKU.WS with reference to AZERTAC, this was stated by the head coach of the Spanish club "Athletic" Ernesto Valverde at the press conference before the third round of the UEFA Champions League group stage against the Agdam club. The specialist emphasized that he respects the "horsemen": [...] The specialist emphasized that he respects the "horsemen": "Of course, they are ahead of us in the standings, while we are at the bottom. Our goal is victory. We respect 'Qarabag' as they have previously defeated strong teams in the Champions League - 'Benfica' and 'Copenhagen'. We are well aware of the opponent's potential. This meeting will be our first this week, so we are fully prepared and full of energy." Note that the match will take place on October 22 at the "San Mames" stadium and will start at 20:45 Baku time. instagram ## Share Mokaland Uşaq Əyləncə Mərkəzi ## Similar News Azerbaijani shooter became the winner of the...

evidences.title: The draw of the Champions 25/26: an unequal path for the five Spanish teams
evidences.content: There’s no doubt the “Yellow Submarine” will need to border on perfection to survive this phase. Still, pressure could become motivation and give them a competitive edge. ## Athletic Club: return with maximum demands Athletic Club returns to the Champions full of excitement, but the calendar gives no respite. At San Mamés they’ll receive PSG, Arsenal, Sporting Lisbon, and Qarabag, while their trips will take them to Dortmund, Bergamo to face Atalanta, Prague against Slavia, and Newcastle in England. A menu packed with high-level rivals that will test the Basque fighting spirit. For Athletic, advancing past this stage would already be a remarkable achievement. ## Comparing levels of difficulty Looking at the schedules, the contrasts are clear: [...] The Champions League 2025-26 draw has been one of the most anticipated in recent years. The renewed 36-team league system definitively replaces the traditional group stage and promises a rollercoaster of emotions. Each club will play eight matches, four at home and four away, against opponents from different pots, with the guarantee of avoiding repeated clashes against clubs from the same country. For Spain, the event was historic: fi...
